title: Translation question
wikitext:
[]: Translation question             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Feb 19, 2008 8:08 am It's always Seele, the hardest lines. Well, about this one at the end of Weva: 死海文書外典は掟の書へと行を移した。 Is it that the scrolls, as written law, advanced one line or as I've heard somewhere, a line is transfered from the scrolls to written law?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Feb 21, 2008 11:36 am Nobody up to this? Where have all our translators gone to? Reichu? Imperialist? Somone? please...?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Fri Feb 22, 2008 3:54 am Dead Sea Scrolls and the Apocrypha is a commandment to write lines moved. I just used the dictionary...

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 22, 2008 7:47 am Oh c'mon. No google translate or bablefish please. Isn't there anyone who knows at least the Japanese required to answer the question on the first post? I mean, Reichu translated the Eva 2 Kimitsu Jouhou, why I'm not hearing bout you here? And wasn't the Imperialist a Japanese guy? Please, help. I have cookies I can give in reward.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Fri Feb 22, 2008 8:03 am protip: offer tits for a greater possibility of translation.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 22, 2008 9:07 am What about DICK&NUTS? Have plenty of them.

[]: Re: Translation question             Quote Post by majlund » Fri Feb 22, 2008 9:22 am AchtungAffen wrote: It's always Seele, the hardest lines. Well, about this one at the end of Weva: 死海文書外典は掟の書へと行を移した。 Is it that the scrolls, as written law, advanced one line or as I've heard somewhere, a line is transfered from the scrolls to written law?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 22, 2008 11:11 am Hmm, I don't think it's (by context) a changed direction. Take a look at the 2 lines (Kaworu and Seele): 「……分かっているよ。あちらの少年が目覚め、概括の段階に入ったんだろ」 「そうだ。死海文書外典は掟の書へと行を移した。契約の時は近い」 Cryptic Kaworu line -> Souda. <cryptic>. The time of the contract is near. That's why I think they're talking about an advancement in their plans, a move forward of one line in the scrolls, the written law. But I'm a noob, need a more experienced oppinion on this.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Feb 22, 2008 1:14 pm Hmm, this is more like First line: "...I understand. The boy there have awakend, and the phase of generalisation (most likely pointing towards the Dead Sea Scrolls, and the things described their being summarised) has started." "The Dead Sea Scrolls' lines have become/turned into a/the Commandment. The time is near (in the same sense as H-Hour)"

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 22, 2008 1:16 pm Many many thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Sat Feb 23, 2008 4:52 am The Imperialist wrote: "The Dead Sea Scrolls' lines have become/turned into a/the Commandment. The time is near (in the same sense as H-Hour)"

[]:             Quote Post by AnonymousEvaTranslator » Sat Feb 23, 2008 8:08 am Particles are weird. "へと" together basically describes the manner in which some change takes place to the proceeding noun, with the addition of a verb after it of course to say what that change is/was. It's "移した" in this case, so The Imperialist is right, it's that the lines of the scrolls have become/transformed into a "commandment(s)." I'm not that familiar with the phrase "Okite no sho," but I know the phrase is in some Buddhist texts, so I'm not sure if it's meant in a religious sense (or at least in a Christian sense), but it probably is.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Feb 25, 2008 5:24 pm Okite on its own means law/rules, so translated literally, would be “The writings of Law” a.k.a Commandments, which should be talking about the Christian one as it mentions the Dead Sea Scrolls. By the way, are you Japanese?

[]:             Quote Post by AnonymousEvaTranslator » Tue Feb 26, 2008 7:11 pm No, I'm not Japanese. I do translations and have for years (The recent script for the Rebuild subs for instance, but other fan-subs and translations as well). I'm just obsessed with Evangelion like everyone else here.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 26, 2008 7:18 pm :looks around casually: Wow, there sure is a lot of Evangelion stuff that needs to be translated!

[]:             Quote Post by AnonymousEvaTranslator » Tue Feb 26, 2008 7:24 pm There is? Well, I'm new here. Point me in the right direction. Seriously though, I wouldn't mind tackling something new. I have some free time.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Feb 26, 2008 7:41 pm I will do it when I stop having this problem of the infected lymphatic nodes constantly pralysing me in strange moments. Should heal in a week or so.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Feb 26, 2008 7:56 pm PM'd both of you, incidentally. (I do like me a nice spot of lymph nodes.....)
